title: Spectral Cascade Ocean
description: An independently implemented inspection path for the reviewed Poseidon mechanisms: deterministic directional JONSWAP/TMA spectra, three disjoint wavelength cascades, packed complex displacement fields, staged two-dimensional GPU IFFTs, choppy derivatives, persistent Jacobian whitecaps, fold-aware normals, shared analytic sky reflection, crest scatter, and filtered micro-detail.

mechanisms: disjoint 250 m, 17 m, and 5 m directional spectrum cascades
independently validated staged inverse FFT architecture
complex packing of horizontal displacement fields
choppy displacement with derivative-map normal reconstruction
shared analytic sky radiance for dome and reflection
fixed ocean camera, fog horizon, and dense displaced surface presentation
divergences: uses WebGL2 fragment render-target FFT stages because the repository gallery Chromium has no WebGPU adapter
uses 64 squared simulation grids for deterministic inspection instead of the reviewed 256 squared WebGPU grids
derivatives are assembled by spatial finite differences after transforming height and horizontal displacement instead of transforming every derivative spectrum
spray particles and live GUI controls are omitted from this focused ocean-system parity surface
